danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct unit sprites and silhouettes for each faction (Protoss crystalline forms, Terran mechanical armor, Zerg organic/chitinous bodies)
danger_signals.meaning: Visual faction differentiation enables instant threat assessment; unfamiliar unit types signal potential danger or new tactical threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Zerg units in mindless, aggressive swarm behavior across contested terrain; creep spread and corrupted environment visuals
danger_signals.meaning: Post-Overmind state creates unpredictable, uncontrolled threat across multiple fronts; visual chaos signals loss of centralized command

danger_signals.visual_form: Invisible or shimmer-effect rendering of dark templar melee units
danger_signals.meaning: Hidden threat; unseen enemy position creates tactical vulnerability and danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Flashing/damage indicators on structures, unit attack animations, smoke/explosion effects
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player economy and defensive position

safety_signals.visual_form: Player-colored unit indicators (typically green/cyan for player perspective); friendly faction silhouettes
safety_signals.meaning: Familiar unit forms indicate allied forces; visual ownership cues establish territory control

safety_signals.visual_form: Fully constructed bunkers, turrets, cannons, and spore colonies with active status visuals
safety_signals.meaning: Fortified positions signal safe zones and damage mitigation capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible mineral patches and vespene geysers with animation; worker units actively harvesting
safety_signals.meaning: Economic security and military capacity buildup

safety_signals.visual_form: Jim Raynor (Terran), Zeratul/Aldaris (Protoss), Kerrigan (Zerg) appearing in briefings and cinematic cutscenes
safety_signals.meaning: Named character presence establishes narrative safety and mission legitimacy

faction_indicators.color_identity: Gold/Yellow and blue in original; cyan/blue in player perspective
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crystalline architecture, angular geometric structures, psionic energy effects
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Darker gold/orange tones for enemy Protoss; corrupted or dim energy visuals
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Aiur homeworld (protoss architecture ruins, alien crystalline flora); tech structures with psionic auras; elevated platforms and archways

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red/dark red for enemy Dominion forces; blue/cyan for player Terran
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Industrial metal structures, mechanical units, military insignia, command centers with satellite dishes
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bright red armor and vehicle plating for hostile Dominion; aggressive stance animations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military bases with command centers, barracks, factory buildings; Koprulu Sector colonial infrastructure; cargo containers and industrial aesthetics

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue/cyan (distinct from Dominion red); Earth origin insignia
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: United Earth Directorate symbols; newer military technology aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player-controlled UED or enemy UED depending on mission context
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Advanced Earth-origin military infrastructure; cleaner, more uniform base designs than Dominion; orbital fleet staging areas

faction_indicators.color_identity: Purple/magenta for player; darker purple/black for enemy swarms
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Organic/biological structures, chitinous carapace textures, pustules and fleshy growths, creep (purple biomass)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dark purple/black coloration; corrupted/mutated appearance; mindless rampage behavior in post-Overmind state
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Creep-covered terrain (purple biological matter spreading), hive clusters, spawning pools, evolutionary chambers; organic architecture with pulsing life indicators

faction_indicators.color_identity: Same purple/magenta as Zerg; Kerrigan-infested aesthetics
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sarah Kerrigan character model (humanoid Terran with Zerg infestation); cerebrate commanding presence
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Organized, controlled Zerg swarm behavior vs. rampaging uncontrolled Zerg
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Organized hive clusters under cerebrate command; strategically positioned creep; structured attack patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Zerg units appear chaotic and uncontrolled; creep spreads erratically; hive clusters show damage and disorganization; visual decay of order
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zerg forces attack mindlessly without strategic coordination; rampage across Aiur unpredictably; cerebrates attempt to regain control (shown through mission briefings and unit behavior changes as player gains control)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Protoss structures damaged or destroyed; alien Zerg biomass corrupting native architecture; sky effects showing conflict; environmental degradation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Protoss defensive operations become more aggressive; alliance of former enemies (Zeratul and Aldaris) shown through joint operations; desperate consolidation tactics

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Terran Dominion threatened by external forces
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dominion military mobilization; red Dominion units increase in presence; fortified positions; command structure visuals showing centralized authority (Arcturus Mengsk)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dominion pursues aggressive expansion; responds to UED and Zerg threats; maintains tight control over colonies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Earth-origin blue/cyan units appearing in Koprulu Sector; new base structures distinct from Dominion red variants; orbital fleet presence indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Strategic position-taking; diplomatic tension with Dominion; coordinated military campaigns; character interactions (DuGalle, Stukov, Duran) show command hierarchy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Samir Duran (mysterious psionic ghost operative) operates in shadows; Raszagal dark templar faction visually distinct from main Protoss (shown through unit cloaking and different armor aesthetics); anti-Dominion rebels appear as player allies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Missions reveal double-agents and hidden agendas; character betrayals and reconciliations change allied faction relationships; mission objectives shift based on hidden factional conflicts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sarah Kerrigan shown as Terran-Zerg hybrid in cutscenes; gradual visual transformation; infested appearance (chitinous growths, purple biomass on humanoid form)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kerrigan commands Zerg forces with intelligence and strategy; represents bridge between Terran mind and Zerg biology; player assumes role of Zerg cerebrate under her command in final campaign

environmental_storytelling.example: Protoss crystalline structures with psionic energy effects contrast sharply with Terran industrial command centers and Zerg organic hive clusters; visual language immediately communicates which faction controls territory
Purple Zerg creep spreading across terrain visually marks Zerg-controlled areas; the extent and pattern of creep spread tells story of Zerg expansion and rampage without dialogue
Structures showing battle damage, burned-out buildings, and destroyed unit remains communicate recent conflict and territorial struggle history
Box art features Sarah Kerrigan; cutscenes between campaign missions reveal character relationships, betrayals, and shifting political alliances; visual narrative complements gameplay missions
New Brood War map tilesets visually represent different locations in Koprulu Sector and Protoss Aiur; terrain types communicate environmental context and resource distribution

environmental_storytelling.technique: AI behavior patterns as narrative communication
environmental_storytelling.example: Increased AI difficulty and improved tactical behavior in Brood War vs. original StarCraft suggests heightened threat and evolved opponent sophistication; visual manifestation through more complex attack patterns and positioning

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Mindless attack patterns; spread across available terrain without strategic objective; chaotic unit clustering; high aggression but low coordination
Defensive positioning; coordinated defensive operations; focus on consolidation rather than expansion; methodical unit deployment against Zerg threat
Aggressive expansion tactics; rapid militarization; strong unit positioning in controlled territories; responds to multiple threats with centralized command structure
Coordinated military campaigns with clear objectives; alliance-building with rebels against Dominion; careful positioning to avoid premature conflict with native factions
Invisible units (cloaking ability); specialization in melee combat; separate from main Protoss force visually; represent ideological opposition to mainstream Protoss tactics
Strategic positioning; organized swarm tactics; purposeful territorial expansion; shift from mindless rampage to coordinated military operations as player gains cerebrate role
Commands Zerg with humanoid intelligence; infested appearance in cutscenes; represents both Terran strategic mind and Zerg biological dominance; narrative role shifts from captive to leader

notes: StarCraft: Brood War communicates world-state primarily through three mechanisms: (1) Faction visual identity via unit design, color schemes, and architecture that enables instant threat assessment; (2) Environmental narrative through creep spread, structural damage, and tileset changes that show territorial control and conflict history; (3) Character/behavior manifestation through cinematic cutscenes and NPC interactions that reveal hidden political tensions (UED vs. Dominion, Kerrigan's rise, factional civil wars). The game's manifestation layer treats faction identity as visually distinct languages—players learn to recognize Protoss crystalline geometry, Terran industrial aesthetics, and Zerg organic biomass as danger/safety signals without explicit UI. The post-Overmind Zerg state serves as central world-state anchor: visual chaos (erratic creep, disorganized units) contrasts with later cerebrate-led order (organized swarms, strategic positioning), communicating player progression and narrative arc. Political situations are communicated through mission objective shifts, character briefings, and revealed alliances (Zeratul-Aldaris reconciliation, UED infiltration, Kerrigan's ascension) rather than explicit factional meters.
